title: Network-based machine learning to identify biomarkers for systemic lupus erythematosus
excerpt: Researchers applied network-based machine learning methods to identify biomarkers for systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE). title: Circulating inflammatory, redox, and apoptosis-related alterations in drug-naive idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis: an exploratory case-control study
excerpt: This exploratory case-control study examined circulating biomarkers related to inflammation, oxidative stress, and apoptosis in treatment-naive patients with idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis compared to controls. The research identified specific alterations in these three pathological pathways that may contribute to disease pathogenesis in IPF. These findings provide preliminary evidence of the molecular mechanisms underlying idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis that could inform future therapeutic development.

title: FLOWR.ROOT - A flow matching-based foundation model for joint multi-purpose structure-aware 3D ligand generation and affinity prediction
excerpt: FLOWR.ROOT is a flow matching-based foundation model that enables joint multi-purpose 3D ligand generation and binding affinity prediction while incorporating structural information about target proteins. The model, developed by researchers at Pfizer's Machine Learning & Computational Sciences and Computational Chemistry divisions, combines ligand design and affinity forecasting capabilities in a single framework. This integrated approach supports structure-aware drug discovery by generating candidate ligands and predicting their binding characteristics simultaneously.

title: Gabapentin Extended Release Tablets in Healthy Subjects Under Fed Conditions: A Randomized, Open-Label, Two-Treatment, Two-Period, Two-Sequence, Single Dose, Crossover, Comparative Bioavailability Study
excerpt: This randomized, open-label, crossover bioavailability study compared gabapentin extended-release tablets in healthy subjects under fed conditions using a two-treatment, two-period, two-sequence design with single-dose administration. The study was conducted by Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Limited and collaborating institutions to establish comparative bioavailability data for the formulation. Published in Drugs R&D, the research provides pharmacokinetic characterization of gabapentin extended-release tablets relevant to regulatory assessment and formulation development.

title: Cytotoxicity and synergistic anticancer activity of Kigelia africana (Lam.) Benth. and Spathodea campanulata P. Beauv. polyherbal extracts
excerpt: This study evaluated the cytotoxic and anticancer properties of polyherbal extracts combining Kigelia africana and Spathodea campanulata, investigating their individual and synergistic effects against cancer cell lines. The research demonstrates that these plant extracts exhibit cytotoxic activity and produce enhanced anticancer effects when used in combination. Findings suggest potential therapeutic applications of these polyherbal formulations in cancer treatment.

title: Ophthalmic self-medication among adult ophthalmic patients attending a tertiary eye care centre in Northwest Ethiopia: a mixed-methods study assessing prevalence, associated factors and lived experience
excerpt: This mixed-methods study evaluated the prevalence of self-medication practices for eye conditions among adult patients at a tertiary eye care centre in Northwest Ethiopia, identifying associated risk factors and documenting patients' firsthand experiences with self-treatment. The research combined quantitative prevalence data with qualitative insights to understand the scope and drivers of ophthalmic self-medication in this population.

title: Safety evaluation of CD3×CD20 bispecific antibodies: a pharmacovigilance study using FDA adverse event reporting system
excerpt: This pharmacovigilance study analyzed adverse event reports from the FDA system to evaluate the safety profile of CD3×CD20 bispecific antibodies, a class of immunotherapeutic agents designed to engage both T cells and B cells in cancer treatment. The research systematically identified and characterized safety signals associated with these bispecific antibodies using real-world post-market surveillance data. The findings provide regulatory and clinical insights into the adverse event patterns and safety considerations for this emerging therapeutic class.

title: Collaborative development of a digital intervention to support opioid tapering after surgery in primary care: an experience-based co-design study with patients and clinicians
excerpt: Researchers used experience-based co-design methodology involving patients and clinicians to develop a digital intervention aimed at supporting opioid tapering in primary care following surgical procedures. The collaborative approach integrated patient and healthcare provider perspectives to create a tool designed to address post-operative opioid management in the primary care setting.

title: FDA Approves New Treatment That Uses Donor Immune Cells to Prevent Serious Complications in Blood Cancer Patients
excerpt: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved TREGZI, the first regulatory T (Treg) cell-based immunotherapy for improving chronic graft-versus-host disease (GVHD)-free survival in adult patients with blood cancers undergoing allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (allo-HSCT).
